course.name: Patient Organizations' Capacity Building - Training Program: The Final Quiz

lessons.title: Recap Module 1: Fundamentals of Effective Advocacy

questions.question: Which skill is essential for building alliances in advocacy work?
Who can be considered as people with lived disease experience?
options: Healthcare professionals involved in treating the disease.
Government officials responsible for disease control.
Individuals living with the disease, their caregivers, families, and advocates.

questions.question: Where do patient organizations engage in advocacy efforts?
options: Media outlets and social media platforms.
Governments, hospital administrators and staff, workplaces, and schools.

questions.question: What is one benefit of having people with lived disease experience in the governance of patient organizations?
options: They can provide financial support for healthcare initiatives
They have extensive medical knowledge and expertise
They have strong connections with pharmaceutical companies
They bring a unique perspective and understanding of patient needs

questions.question: What is the main focus of individual-driven advocacy?
options: Supporting organizations in advocating for policy change
Empowering individuals to advocate for their own rights
Providing resources for collective advocacy efforts
Promoting community-driven initiatives for social change

questions.question: What is one of the key skills required for effective advocacy?
What is the main difference between advocacy and effective advocacy?
options: Advocacy is focused on raising awareness only.
Effective advocacy is more expensive than regular advocacy.
Advocacy and effective advocacy have the same goals and outcomes.

lessons.title: Recap Module 2: Fundraising Strategies that Drive Results

questions.question: Is the following statement true or false?
questions.statement: Matching gift arrangements, with the company matching any dollars donated by their employees with an equivalent donation is an example of corporate partnership.

questions.question: Is the following statement true or false?
questions.statement: Major gifts are the largest donations an organization receives from a single source in a single fiscal year.

questions.question: Is the following statement true or false?
questions.statement: In Peer-to-Peer fundraising, donors have direct access to friends, family members, coworkers, and other peers that may have never heard of your organization.

lessons.title: Recap Module 3: Volunteer Management
lessons.capture_note: Slides 1-5 captured. Slides 6-11 not yet captured.

questions.question: What else is volunteer engagement referred to as:
Which of the following is NOT one of the four key activities in the recruitment phase?
What is an effective way to provide orientation to new volunteers?
options: Conduct a formal training session once a year
Give them a volunteer handbook and leave them to read it on their own.
Have them start working immediately without any introduction
Assign a mentor or partner, such as a senior volunteer or a leader

questions.question: Who is responsible for recognizing volunteers within an organization?
What is a key trend in volunteering that organizations should be aware of?
options: Volunteers are less interested in organizational goals
Volunteers are increasingly seeking short-term involvement

lessons.title: Recap Module 6: Effective Communication
lessons.capture_note: Slides 12-16 captured. Slides 1-11 not yet captured.

questions.question: How can team leaders effectively empower team members?
options: By micromanaging their tasks and decisions.
By providing them with authority and autonomy to make decisions.
By restricting their access to information and resources.

questions.question: What is a key aspect of building a culture of inclusivity?
options: Embracing different perspectives and encouraging collaboration.
Ignoring biases and discrimination in the workplace.
Focusing on individual achievements rather than teamwork.
Limiting growth and development opportunities to a select few.

questions.question: What is one strategy for maintaining fruitful relationships with partners from diverse cultural backgrounds?
options: Using complex language and technical jargon.
Adapting your communication style to accommodate cultural norms and preferences.
Speaking quickly to ensure meetings end promptly.
Being inflexible in your communication approach.

questions.question: What is the recommended approach for encouraging participation from a silent audience member?
options: Invite everyone to share their opinions and respond positively when they contribute.
Ask them direct questions and pressure them to respond.
Criticize their lack of participation in front of the group to motivate them.
Thank them for their attention and move on to other participants.

questions.question: How can nonverbal communication enhance the effectiveness of your speech?
options: By using filler words to fill pauses in speech.
By conveying confidence, sincerity, and engagement.
By distracting the audience from the main message.
By speaking in a monotone voice to maintain neutrality.

lessons.title: Recap Module 7: Development of Impactful Strategic Work Plans

questions.question: Is the following statement true or false?
questions.statement: The community you serve is a stakeholder.

questions.question: Is the following statement true or false?
questions.statement: One key part of a concept note is the problem you are trying to address.

questions.question: Is the following statement true or false?
questions.statement: The rationale includes what is being achieved.

questions.question: What is work plan implementation?
options: Monitoring project progress is crucial. As the project nears completion, prepare to present a comprehensive evaluation to stakeholders. Highlight achievements and outline steps for future projects to sustain and build upon this work.
The detailed work plan outlines the process from concept to execution, enabling users to understand the project's success measurement.
Executing the work plan is vital for achieving desired outcomes and communicating success to stakeholders. Monitoring ensures goals are met.

questions.question: What is the work plan cycle in order?
options: Recruitment, concept note, work plan development, implementation, evaluation
Concept note, evaluation, work plan development, implementation
Concept note, work plan development, implementation, evaluation
Concept note, work plan development, implementation

questions.question: What should be worked on after the concept note has been approved?
What is the difference between a concept note and a work plan?
options: A work plan is for getting stakeholders while a concept note is how this will be achieved
A concept note is for getting stakeholders while a work plan is how this will be achieved

questions.question: Is the following statement true or false?
questions.statement: Less documentation means your organization will be stronger.

questions.question: Is the following statement true or false?
questions.statement: Success can only be measured through a survey.

questions.question: What is most important?
options: Having a budget, timeline and ensuring success
Getting your project out as soon as possible
Making the most amount of money possible

questions.question: Is the following statement true or false?
questions.statement: The logical framework does not help see ways to measure success.

questions.question: What are some of the parts of a logical framework?
options: This is where you decide the reason of the activity
Only looks at the amount of participants and trainees

questions.question: Why is documenting important?
options: It is only needed for donors to see success
Helps to ensure accountability, betters communication and collaboration, legal reasons

questions.question: Is the following statement true or false?
questions.statement: Do not allow for scope creep.

questions.question: What does the 'A' in SMART-IE mean?
Is the following statement true or false?
questions.statement: When working on work plan, expand the rationale.

questions.question: Is the following statement true or false?
questions.statement: The rationale includes what is being achieved.

questions.question: Is the following statement true or false?
questions.statement: Projects help programs to achieve its aims.

questions.question: Is the following statement true or false?
questions.statement: Capital grants can also be used for operations.

questions.question: Is the following statement true or false?
questions.statement: General operating grants are always restricted.

questions.question: Is the following statement true or false?
questions.statement: Reporting after receiving funding is not very important.

questions.question: How likely are you to recommend the course content (e.g., relevance, clarity, and depth) to others?
How likely are you to say the course helped you achieve your learning goals?
How likely are you to say the course pace was manageable and allowed you to keep up with the material?
How likely are you to say the course instructions and expectations were clear and easy to follow?
How likely are you to use what you've learned from this course in real-world scenarios?
What other topics would you want to learn about in future modules?
Any other comments or feedback?
